description_long: This is a robust sledgehammer featuring a thick, rectangular metal head with flat striking faces on both ends. The head is solid and appears to be made of steel, showing a slightly matte, worn finish. The handle is straight, cylindrical, and made of wood, with a natural light brown color and a smooth surface. The junction between the head and handle is tight, with no visible fasteners or additional grip enhancements. The hammer is utilitarian in design, lacking any decorative elements or branding. Its overall appearance suggests it is built for heavy-duty tasks such as breaking concrete or driving stakes in urban construction or maintenance settings.
description: A heavy-duty sledgehammer with a solid steel head and a straight wooden handle, designed for demanding urban tasks. The tool is simple, sturdy, and shows a utilitarian finish.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full width of the rectangular metal head and the straight wooden handle beneath it.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the narrow profile of the hammer head and the cylindrical shape of the handle.
description_view_2: Rear view mirroring the front, again displaying the broad face of the hammer head and the handle.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 1, showing the slim edge of the head and the handle's roundness.
attribute_handle_type: Straight wooden handle, cylindrical, smooth, with a natural finish and no visible grip enhancements.
attribute_head_type: Rectangular sledgehammer head, double flat striking faces, solid steel, no claws or peen.
